questions.q: If two positive integers a and b are written as a = x³y² and b = xy³, where x, y are prime numbers, then HCF(a, b) is:
questions.explain: We take the lowest power of each common prime factor: x¹ and y², giving HCF = xy².

questions.q: The LCM of two numbers is 1200. Which of the following cannot be their HCF?
questions.explain: HCF must be a perfect factor of LCM. 1200 is not divisible by 500, so 500 cannot be the HCF.

questions.q: If n is any natural number, then 6ⁿ - 5ⁿ always ends with the digit:
questions.explain: 6ⁿ always ends in 6 and 5ⁿ always ends in 5, so their difference always ends in 1.

questions.q: The product of a non-zero rational number and an irrational number is always:
questions.explain: The product is always guaranteed to be irrational.

questions.q: If the HCF of 65 and 117 is expressible in the form 65m - 117, then the value of m is:
